description: Structured logging field catalog for GrantLayer observability baseline. Defines required, optional, forbidden, and redacted fields. This is a design artifact; no logging is implemented.

requiredFields.format: ISO 8601 with timezone offset or UTC (Z)

requiredFields.description: Short human-readable description of the event.
Event category from the observability event model.

correlationFields.description: Ties all log entries generated during a single HTTP/API request.
Higher-level identifier spanning multiple requests (batch, callback, etc.).
Ties all steps of a single GrantLayer workflow.
Ties all operations within a single execution instance.
Identity of the human or system performing the action.
Specific agent or service instance that handled the request.

optionalFields.description: File path relative to repository root.
Runtime thread identifier for concurrency debugging.
Machine-readable error code for ERROR-level events.
Classification: transient, permanent, internal, external, retryable, fatal.
Structured context object for event-specific data. Dynamic keys allowed inside this object only.

futureFields.description: Tenant or organization identifier. NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. Only to be added when multi-tenant boundaries are explicitly designed.

forbiddenFields.description: Any raw secret value (password, API secret, private key material). Must never appear in logs.
Bearer tokens, JWT bodies, session cookies, refresh tokens. Must never appear in logs.
Ed25519 signing keys, TLS private keys, SSH keys. Must never appear in logs.
Raw evidence content or large sensitive evidence documents. Must be referenced by ID or hash only.
Personal identifiers, financial account numbers, contact details. Only allowed if explicitly permitted by future data classification policy.
Database connection strings containing credentials. Must never appear in logs.
Raw SQL queries. Use operation type labels instead.

metadata.note: No logging library, metrics backend, or tracing system is implemented. This catalog defines the intended schema for future implementation.
